channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 6 people killed in Tel Aviv shooting attack
item.description: Six civilians were killed Tuesday in a shooting in Tel Aviv, local police said, as tensions between Israel and Iran-backed terrorist groups in the region have dramatically escalated in recent days and weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: What Iran attacks mean for Americans in Israel: Vittert
item.description: NewsNation's Leland Vittert believes there will not be immediate panic for most of the more than 500,000 Americans living in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Hezbollah's capabilities after its recent losses
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah has suffered some of the heaviest losses in its history over the past two weeks, chief among them the killing of its longtime leader, Hassan Nasrallah, in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How does Israel’s Iron Dome work?
item.description: Iran launched missiles toward Israel Tuesday, just days after Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed in a strike on Lebanon. The Iranian blitz comes after a nearly yearlong cross-border conflict between Hezbollah, a star proxy group for Tehran, and Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Graham: Iranian missile attack on Israel deserves 'overwhelming response' from US
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) responded to Iran’s launch of a missile on Israel and said the attack deserves an “overwhelming response” from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: France's new prime minister vows to curb debt as he faced baptism of fire at heckled parliament
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Lawmakers gave new French Prime Minister Michel Barnier a rough ride, heckling him constantly, as he laid out his proposed policies in an inaugural speech to parliament on Tuesday, a baptism of fire that highlighted how difficult governing will be for his minority government.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Japan's new premier vows to push strong defense under Japan-US alliance and boost economy
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The newly elected Japanese prime minister pledged Tuesday night to stick to the vital Japan-U.S. alliance amid growing tension in the region while calling for it to be more equitable.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran launches missile attack on Israel
item.description: Iran launched a barrage of missiles at Israel on Tuesday, shortly after the U.S. warned an attack was imminent. The attack appeared to be over within an hour of starting.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels launch drone boat that hits ship in Red Sea as missile strikes another
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels launched an explosive-loaded drone that crashed into one ship Tuesday in the Red Sea and a missile that exploded against another.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Embassy in Israel warns employees to shelter in place amid threat of Iran missile attack
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Jerusalem is cautioning all government employees and their family members to shelter in place until further notice as Iran prepares to launch missiles at Israel. The warning came as U.S. officials said they expected Iran to attack Israel with missile and drone strikes on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: IDF meets little resistance from Hezbollah after weeks of hitting terror targets, officials say
item.description: The Israel Defense Force met little resistance from Hezbollah terrorists despite weeks of growing anticipation of an Israeli incursion amid strikes and raids by specials ops units in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Iran's missile barrage and Israel's ground operations in Lebanon
item.description: Iran launched almost 200 missiles at Israel, though Israeli authorities said there were no injuries and scattered damage and fires from falling shrapnel.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A cruise ship bound on a round-the-world voyage leaves Belfast after a 4-month delay
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A cruise ship taking passengers on a 3 ½-year around-the-world voyage has finally sailed from Belfast's dockside, after spending four months stuck in the Northern Ireland capital.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tibetan group in India protests against China and human rights situation in homeland
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Dozens of Tibetans living in India protested outside China's embassy on Tuesday against the human rights situation in their homeland, which China annexed in 1951.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China marks 75 years of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger over the massive state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel begins ground offensive against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli ground forces crossed into southern Lebanon early Tuesday, marking a significant escalation of an offensive against Hezbollah militants and opening a new front in a yearlong war against its Iranian-backed adversaries.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, ABBY SEWELL and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indian-controlled Kashmir votes in final phase of polls to elect local government
item.description: Voting has begun in Indian-controlled Kashmir in the final phase of an election to choose a local government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 3 police officers sentenced to prison over the Halloween crush in South Korea that killed nearly 160
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court gave three police officers prison sentences on Monday over their botched handling of a 2022 Halloween crush in a Seoul nightlife district that killed nearly 160 people.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel launches limited ground operations in Lebanon as war against Hezbollah, terrorist groups continue
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) began limited operations in Lebanon on Monday evening, continuing its battle against Hezbollah terrorists. The military killed several key figures of the group over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN extends Haiti mission mandate after dropping push for larger peacekeeping effort
item.description: The United Nations Security Council on Monday unanimously renewed a multinational security support mission (MSS) in Haiti to combat armed gangs after the U.S. dropped a push to enlarge the effort into a peacekeeping force. The resolution, led by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israeli attacks on Hezbollah roil Middle East: 5 pressing questions
item.description: Israel has decimated Hezbollah’s leadership with strikes over the past week, while also killing hundreds of civilians and forcing nearly 1 million people from their homes.
item.dc:creator: Colin Meyn, The Hill

item.title: Israel's ground invasion into Lebanon imminent as cabinet approves next phase of the war
item.description: President Biden demanded a 'ceasefire now' following reports that Washington had been briefed by Israel on its plans to launch a 'limited' ground invasion into Lebanon imminently in fight with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah says it will choose Nasrallah's successor 'at the earliest opportunity'
item.description: Hezbollah’s second-in-command, deputy leader Naim Qassem, says the terrorist group will choose its new leader "at the earliest opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 148 are dead in floods and landslides in Nepal
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Rescuers in Nepal recovered dozens of bodies from buses and other vehicles that were buried in landslides near the capital Kathmandu, as the death toll from flooding rose to at least 148 with dozens missing, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli Air Force strikes Houthi targets in Yemen with 'extensive' operation
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces said the Israeli Air Force struck Houthi terror targets, including power plants and a seaport used to import oil, in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flooding and landslides in Nepal kill at least 66 people, with as many again still missing
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Flooding and landslides caused by continuous rainfall has killed at least 66 people in Nepal, and a further 69 are missing, officials said Saturday. Rain has been pouring down since Friday night and is expected to continue over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A far-right party is looking for a historic election win in Austria
item.description: Austria's far-right Freedom Party could win its first national election when the country votes on Sunday, but polls point to a close race in the parliamentary election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu vows to use 'full force' against Hezbollah and dims hopes for a cease-fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday vowed to carry out “full force” strikes against Hezbollah until it ceases firing rockets across the border, dimming hopes for a cease-fire proposal put forth by U.S. and European officials.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, BASSEM MROUE and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
